steps.title: Identify what the blank must supply
steps.content: The sentence is: The collection ______ 18 plays that might otherwise have been lost... The subject The collection is singular. The blank is the main verb of the sentence, so it must be a finite (tensed) verb that agrees with a singular subject — not an infinitive or participle, which cannot function as a main verb.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘to be including’: A to-infinitive cannot serve as a main verb; the sentence would have no finite verb. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘including’: A present participle cannot serve as a main verb without an auxiliary; the sentence would be a fragment. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘to include’: Another to-infinitive — same problem as A, no finite verb. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘includes’: Third-person singular present tense — a proper finite verb that agrees with the singular subject “The collection.” ✓
That confirms D is correct.
